title: funkiella durangensis subsp. tenella
intro: Funkiella durangensis subsp. tenella, also known as Mexican evening primrose, is a plant species belonging to the family Onagraceae, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been found to exhibit medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Funkiella durangensis subsp. tenella include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
